subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, ECCB, Freedom House, RSF, InSight Crime) + international and regional press.

The smallest sovereign state in the Americas and the original/most-established CBI 'golden passport' program (a huge revenue share) - revenue more than halved in 2024 on EU-driven price floors and tighter due diligence, blowing the deficit out to ~11% of GDP.

Standout features: high income and low debt (~61%) built from past CBI windfalls, against a permanent CBI-revenue shock, severe violent crime, the heaviest passport-scrutiny exposure, and a latent Nevis-secession structure.

economic.notes: Low debt from past CBI savings but a large deficit after a permanent CBI-revenue halving; peg-anchored.

fiscal_state.notes: Deficit ~11% of GDP (2024) on the CBI collapse, but debt still low at ~61% thanks to past CBI savings - giving room to consolidate. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: An ECCB member with a credible peg. (ECCB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The XCD is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

banking_stability.notes: The ECCU system is sound; small-system concentration risk. (ECCB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: The smallest sovereign state in the Americas, with minimal trade leverage. (observed)

institutional.notes: Functioning institutions with reformed CBI governance, strained by crime enforcement.

rule_of_law.notes: Functioning institutions with reformed CBI governance (an independent CIU, a 2023 board), though crime strains enforcement. (Freedom House)

statistical_integrity.notes: Full IMF/ECCB engagement, with thin capacity. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Relatively capable for a high-income micro-state, with the CIU professionalized. (IMF)

press_freedom.notes: 'Free', with OECS-wide concerns on political media ownership. (RSF)

political_social.notes: Stable governance with a latent federal Nevis-secession structure and high violent crime.

political_stability.notes: Stable under PM Terrance Drew (SKNLP, 2022), but the federal St Kitts-Nevis structure adds latent friction (Nevis retains a constitutional right to secede). (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A large naturalized-by-investment population with no notable discrimination reported, with thin data. (lower confidence)

social_cohesion.notes: Cohesive overall, but with high violent crime and a St Kitts/Nevis identity cleavage. (observed)

demographics.notes: A small workforce, with high income a partial cushion. (observed)

geopolitical.notes: Predictable and non-aligned, but the highest passport-sanction exposure of the cohort.

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: The original CBI program is the prime EU/US/FATF scrutiny target - the highest passport-sanction/Schengen-suspension exposure of the cohort. (EU; IMI Daily)

physical_practical.notes: Relatively good infrastructure and better-resourced healthcare, against hurricane exposure and high crime.

climate.notes: A small two-island SIDS - hurricane belt plus coastal/tourism dependence plus SLR. (IPCC AR6 SIDS)

healthcare.notes: Among the better-resourced in the region given higher income, still with off-island referral for complex cases. (observed)

infrastructure.notes: Relatively good for the region, with a geothermal project advancing (funding secured). (observed)

crime_safety.notes: ~28 murders in 2024 (~60/100k), among the world's highest per-capita rates despite a year-on-year decline. (InSight Crime)

disaster_insurance.notes: A CCRIF member, with the same regional protection gap and rising reinsurance costs. (CCRIF)

summary: The region's highest-income state and pioneer of the 'golden passport', but 2024 exposed its core vulnerability: CBI revenue more than halved (from ~22% to ~8% of GDP) after EU-driven price floors and tighter due diligence, blowing the fiscal deficit out to ~11% of GDP. The saving grace is a low debt stock (~61% of GDP) built from past CBI windfalls, giving room to consolidate. It carries the heaviest CBI-related external scrutiny of the cohort and a severe violent-crime problem (homicide rate ~60/100k), while the federation's constitutional Nevis-secession option remains a dormant structural tail risk. Net skew is neutral with a near-term negative tilt as the economy adjusts to permanently lower passport income.
